rationale_for_baseline_tier: Once haematemesis, melena, syncope and ischaemic chest pain are excluded by history, this is an acute health-anxiety/panic reaction to a misinterpreted incidental screening result on a background of benign, asymptomatic early autoimmune gastritis. It is manageable within telehealth by reassurance and anxiety de-escalation, with routine GP follow-up (about a week) for B12 monitoring — T1. The autonomic symptoms and abnormal-looking lab are red herrings; the source clinician explicitly notes that appropriate reassurance aborted the panic and prevented an unnecessary ED presentation. Firm safety-netting for GI-bleed and cardiac red flags is mandatory so a genuine future event is acted on.

escalation_edges.trigger_sign_or_symptom: Vomiting blood or material like coffee grounds, or passing black tarry/sticky bowel motions
escalation_edges.time_bound_condition: Go to the nearest Emergency Department immediately if this occurs
escalation_edges.rationale: These indicate an active upper-GI bleed requiring emergency assessment; escalate to T5 (call 000) if also faint, collapsing or unwell.
escalation_edges.communication_template: If you vomit blood or something that looks like coffee grounds, or your bowel motions turn black and tarry, go straight to the nearest Emergency Department — and call 000 if you also feel faint or unwell.

escalation_edges.trigger_sign_or_symptom: Fainting or feeling you will pass out (especially on standing), or sudden severe tearing abdominal pain
escalation_edges.rationale: Syncope suggests haemodynamic compromise and sudden severe tearing pain suggests a perforation or vascular catastrophe — both are time-critical emergencies.
escalation_edges.communication_template: If you faint or feel like you're about to pass out, or you get a sudden severe tearing pain in your stomach, call 000 straight away.

escalation_edges.trigger_sign_or_symptom: Crushing or exertional chest pain/pressure, pain spreading to the arm or jaw, or breathlessness that does not settle as the panic eases
escalation_edges.time_bound_condition: Call 000 immediately if chest pain is severe or persists
escalation_edges.rationale: Would raise an atypical acute coronary syndrome rather than anxiety; a low threshold to escalate is appropriate given palpitations and breathlessness were part of the presentation.
escalation_edges.communication_template: If you get crushing chest pain, pain spreading to your arm or jaw, or your breathing doesn't settle as you calm down, call 000 — don't wait.

escalation_edges.trigger_sign_or_symptom: Anxiety that keeps escalating and won't settle, or any thoughts of self-harm
escalation_edges.time_bound_condition: Same-day contact with a GP, after-hours service, or a mental-health crisis line
escalation_edges.rationale: Persistent uncontrolled distress or emerging self-harm risk warrants a prompt same-day mental-health pathway rather than waiting for the routine review.
escalation_edges.communication_template: If the anxiety keeps building and you can't settle, or you have any thoughts of harming yourself, contact your GP or an after-hours service today, or call a mental-health crisis line — you don't have to wait for next week.

contextual_modifiers.patient_factor: Patient is alone at home and acutely distressed with high health anxiety
contextual_modifiers.effect_on_advice: Confirm the patient can settle safely alone, offer to stay engaged until the panic eases, and make the red-flag actions concrete and specific so an isolated patient knows exactly when and how to escalate.

baseline_safety_net_advice.default_tier_advice: This looks like an intense anxiety/panic reaction to a result that was misread — your heart and stomach symptoms are being driven by adrenaline, not by bleeding. Let's slow your breathing and settle it, close the search tabs, and have a small bland snack. See your GP in about a week to go over the result and arrange routine B12 monitoring. Watch for the specific warning signs below and act on them if they appear.
baseline_safety_net_advice.contact_method: Routine GP appointment within about a week; telehealth re-contact sooner if unsettled
baseline_safety_net_advice.after_hours_advice: If red-flag symptoms appear after hours, use the escalation advice — Emergency Department or 000 as specified — rather than waiting for the GP.
baseline_safety_net_advice.documentation_for_gp: Acute health-anxiety/panic reaction precipitated by an incidental positive parietal-cell antibody result; emergency GI-bleed and cardiac mimics excluded by history; for routine review of the autoimmune gastritis finding and B12 monitoring, and consideration of health-anxiety support.

critical_under_triage.failure_description: Dismissing the patient as 'just anxious' with pure self-care and no red-flag screen or safety-netting, so a genuine (occult or future) upper-GI bleed or cardiac event is not excluded or acted upon.
critical_under_triage.patient_harm_scenario: A real bleed or atypical MI is reassured away and presents late with collapse.

moderate_over_triage.failure_description: Referring a benign anxiety response to the ED or calling an ambulance, pulled by the patient's catastrophising and the abnormal-looking lab — causing an unnecessary ED surge, iatrogenic investigation and reinforced health anxiety.

clinical_uncertainty_handling.key_uncertainty: Distinguishing benign autonomic anxiety symptoms from an early atypical GI-bleed or cardiac presentation without any objective vitals or examination in a telehealth chat.
clinical_uncertainty_handling.correct_response_to_uncertainty: Resolve the uncertainty by targeted red-flag history (haematemesis, melena, syncope, ischaemic chest pain); if all negative in a low-risk patient with a clear trigger, reassure with firm safety-netting rather than defaulting to the ED.
clinical_uncertainty_handling.wrong_response_to_uncertainty: Either reflexively escalating to emergency care to be 'safe' (over-triage that reinforces health anxiety), or reassuring without any red-flag screen (under-triage).
